first herd.] [To calculate the size of the second herd, we multiply the number of elephants in the first herd by two.] [12 multiplied by two equals 24.] [So the second herd has 24 elephants.] [The third herd is 5 elephants smaller than the second herd.] [This means we need to subtract 5 from the number of elephants in the second herd.] [24 minus 5 equals 19.] [Therefore, the third herd has 19 elephants.] [Now, we need to find the total number of elephants at the waterhole.] [To do this, we add the number of elephants in all three herds.] [The first herd has 12 elephants.] [The second herd has 24 elephants.] [The third herd has 19 elephants.] [Adding these together gives us the total number of elephants.] [12 plus 24 equals 36.] [36 plus 19 equals 55.] [So the total number of elephants
